parts.text:
In the sparkling, sun-drenched waters of Bikini Bottom, lived SpongeBob SquarePants. His laugh was as bubbly as a soda, and his heart was as big as the ocean. Most days were spent flipping Krabby Patties at the Krusty Krab, jellyfishing with Patrick, and trying, usually in vain, to get a smile out of his grumpy neighbor, Squidward.

But one day, a most peculiar, and rather sparkly, thing happened.

Above the Krusty Krab, the water rippled and swirled. A shimmering portal, like a kaleidoscope made of stardust, spun open! From it tumbled not a rogue Krabby Patty or a stray jellyfish, but something entirely new. It landed with a soft *thump* right at SpongeBob’s feet.

SpongeBob froze, his spatula forgotten mid-flip. His square eyes widened. Jaw slightly slack, he just stared.

Before him stood a creature of pure, vibrant color. Her mane flowed like spun rainbow silk, and her eyes twinkled like freshly polished stars. She looked around, a little unsteady on her hooves, her expression uncertain.

“Oh, barnacles!” SpongeBob finally managed, his voice a squeak.

The creature blinked, then offered a gentle smile. “Hello?” she said, her voice like tinkling bells. “I seem to be a bit lost.”

She introduced herself as Prismia. She was a Little Pony from a land far away. She’d been exploring, perhaps a little too close to a strange, shimmering gateway, and now… now she found herself in a world of water and coral. Prismia looked around, her star-bright eyes taking in the strange, bubbly surroundings with a mix of wonder and confusion.

SpongeBob was utterly captivated. He had never seen such a bright, joyful creature, even if she did look a little bewildered. Prismia, in turn, was fascinated by SpongeBob's boundless enthusiasm. She’d never met anyone who could find so much delight in a simple task like flipping a burger.

“Your spatula work!” Prismia exclaimed, her hooves tapping lightly on the sandy floor. “It’s like a dance! And your laugh! It’s so… *zesty*!”

SpongeBob’s usually bright yellow cheeks turned a rosy pink. “Why, thank you, Prismia! You… you have the most amazing colors I’ve ever seen! They’re brighter than any jellyfish I’ve ever caught!”

He couldn’t wait to show her everything! He took her jellyfishing, but Prismia was a little hesitant about the stinging tentacles. With a quick *whoosh* and a clever flick of his net, SpongeBob expertly guided her away from any danger, showing her how to catch them safely. He even tried to teach her how to blow bubbles. But Prismia’s breath was too gentle. The bubbles just floated away like tiny, iridescent balloons, too shy to grow large.

One afternoon, while they were sitting on a bed of soft, swaying sea anemones, SpongeBob felt a strange flutter in his chest. It was a new feeling, warm and wobbly. When Prismia would smile, his heart would do a little flip. When she’d giggle, he felt warm all over, like he’d just eaten a dozen Krabby Patties.

“Prismia,” he began, his voice a little shaky, “I… I think I really like you.”

Prismia tilted her head, her starlike eyes curious. “Like me? You mean, like how you like Krabby Patties?”

SpongeBob blushed deeper. “Well, yes, I *really* like Krabby Patties. But this is… this is different. This is like wanting to share every sunrise with you. Like wanting to make you laugh every single day. It’s… it’s like my heart has grown an extra bubble, and it’s all because of you!”

Prismia’s eyes widened, and a soft blush spread across her own colorful coat. She understood. This feeling, this special sparkle that made her heart sing with joy and warmth… in her home, they called this powerful friendship magic.

“Oh, SpongeBob,” she whispered, her voice full of warmth. “I feel it too! Your kindness, your laughter… it makes my whole world feel brighter. I think… I think this magic makes me love you too.”

They spent many happy days together. SpongeBob learned to appreciate the beauty of a perfectly formed cloud, and Prismia learned the exquisite joy of a perfectly cooked Krabby Patty (though she mostly just admired SpongeBob making them). He showed her the wonders of Bikini Bottom, and she showed him how to find the magic in even the simplest things.

But the portal that brought Prismia to Bikini Bottom was unstable. One day, it began to shimmer and swell again, growing larger and brighter. Prismia knew it was time to go home. A tear, like a tiny diamond, welled up in her eye.

“Oh, SpongeBob,” she said, her voice sad. “The gateway is open again. I have to go back.”

SpongeBob’s cheerful demeanor faltered. “But… but I don’t want you to leave! Who will I share my bubble-blowing adventures with? Who will tell me my spatula work is like a ballet?”

Prismia nudged him gently with her nose. “Don’t worry, SpongeBob. We will always have our memories. And perhaps, if the magic is strong enough, the portal will open again someday.” She gave him a final, loving nuzzle. “And remember, true friendship, and love, can sparkle across any distance.”

With a final, wistful look at SpongeBob, Prismia stepped into the swirling portal. It closed with a gentle *poof*, leaving only a faint scent of rainbow and a sprinkle of glitter on the ocean floor.

SpongeBob stood there for a moment, a little sad but also filled with a new, warm feeling. He looked at the remaining glitter, a reminder of his colorful friend. He knew that even though Prismia was gone, the love he felt for her would stay in his heart, making every Krabby Patty he flipped, and every jellyfish he caught, just a little bit more sparkly. And he couldn't help but hope that one day, the kaleidoscope portal might shimmer open again.
